name: Mexican Guerrero Highlands (Malinaltepec, ~1980m)

staple_foods.name: Arabica coffee (Bourbon, Guerrero highlands)

key_flavor_bioactives.polyphenol_content: 5350-5800 mg/100g CGA equivalents [Bourbon cv., Guerrero highlands 1980m — Gallardo-Ignacio et al. 2023 Molecules 28:4685; green bean dry weight; Bourbon 5575±231 mg/100g]
key_flavor_bioactives.caffeine_concentration: 10.0-13.0 mg/g dry bean [Guerrero highland Arabica — Gallardo-Ignacio et al. 2023; Bourbon cultivar at 1980m]

staple_foods.notes: Arabica coffee from the Región de la Montaña, Guerrero State, Mexico — specifically municipalities of La Soledad and Paraje Montero, Malinaltepec, at 1980m. Three cultivars were studied by Gallardo-Ignacio et al. (2023): Bourbon (5575±231 mg/100g CGA), Oro Azteca (5463±243 mg/100g), and Typica (3681±10 mg/100g). This RSU uses Bourbon as the primary cultivar for comparability with other high-quality Arabica natural experiment RSUs. The Bourbon range (5350-5800 mg/100g) is consistent with altitude expectations: between Kenyan AA at 1800m (~4750 midpoint) and Ethiopian highland at 2200m (~5350 midpoint). Guerrero highland Arabica is shade-grown under cloud forest at 1700-2100m — among the highest-altitude Mexican coffee zones. This is the first Mexican RSU in the dataset and adds a North American/Mesoamerican data point to the cross-continental coffee altitude gradient.
data_sources: Gallardo-Ignacio et al. (2023) — Chemical and Biological Characterization of Green and Processed Coffee Beans from Coffea arabica Varieties, Molecules 28(12):4685

notes: Guerrero State highland coffee zone (~1980m, Región de la Montaña). Metamorphic mountain soils, cloud forest microclimate. Coffee is the primary cash crop for indigenous Mixtec and Nahua communities in this region. Grown at 1700-2100m under shade trees — among the highest-altitude Arabica zones in Mexico. Green bean CGA data from Gallardo-Ignacio et al. (2023, Molecules) — open access PMC article providing direct green bean dry weight measurements. Fills the altitude gap between RSU-27 (Kenya 1800m) and RSU-17 (Ethiopia 2200m) with a geographically independent (North American) data point.
